topic: Analyze opencode-lcm memory injection patterns for OpenCode and determine how to apply them to packet 024 compact code graph work, plus identify broader Spec Kit Memory improvements worth porting.

focus: Map opencode-lcm plugin architecture and runtime insertion points
summary: Confirmed the plugin is an OpenCode lifecycle adapter, not just a search library: it captures runtime events, exposes archive tools, injects system hints, injects synthetic recalled context into chat messages, and appends a compaction note during experimental session compaction. Core evidence came from README, index.ts, and the transform tests.

focus: Trace storage, retrieval, ranking, privacy, and repair mechanics inside opencode-lcm
summary: Confirmed a parallel SQLite archive with sessions/messages/parts/resumes/artifacts/artifact_blobs/summary_nodes/summary_edges/summary_state plus three FTS tables. Retrieval uses phrase-aware FTS, TF-IDF token filtering, per-scope budgets, result-type quotas, recency-aware reranking, and doctor-driven repair of summary/index/lineage drift.

focus: Compare LCM mechanics with packet 024 compact code graph and current Spec Kit Memory surfaces
summary: Confirmed packet 024 already prefers clean-room graph + existing memory stack over foreign dependencies, and our current system already has hook-based lifecycle support, startup instruction digests, auto-surfaced memories, working-memory attention boosts, code_graph_query/context tools, and a separate SQLite graph store design. The main gap is OpenCode-native prompt-time injection, not core retrieval logic.

focus: Synthesize adoption strategy and memory-system improvement shortlist
summary: Converged on a layered recommendation: build an OpenCode-specific adapter plugin that borrows LCM's insertion points but uses our own memory/code-graph retrieval as the source of truth; do not fork its full archive as our main memory backend. Broader platform improvements worth porting are repair tooling, deduplicated blob storage, privacy pre-index filters, portable archive snapshots, and expandable summary DAGs for large archives.

focus: Compare LCM operational patterns against current code-graph, hook-state, and compaction runtime surfaces
summary: Confirmed our current graph runtime already has compact startup/compaction builders, structural bootstrap, and budget-aware merge/deduplication, but it lacks several operational capabilities shown by LCM: workspace-bounded path resolution, normalized worktree identity, portable snapshot export/import with rehome logic, pin-aware retention, and metadata-only artifact preview providers.

focus: Turn broader graph/hook comparison into specific implementation recommendations
summary: Converged on broader recommendations for the existing graph runtime: add a code_graph_doctor surface, add graph-adjacent snapshot export/import, enrich startup and compaction payload provenance, adopt explicit workspace/worktree safety for any new file-based operations, and keep the current compact-merger and structural-bootstrap design as the foundation.

focus: Startup, resume, and provenance behavior
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass clarified that startup and resume need both surface provenance and data provenance, introduced an imported-unverified to rebuilt to ready lifecycle for future imports, corrected the relative-safe but absolute-portable path nuance, and highlighted lineage/worktree/working-set fields as the main startup/resume payload gap.

focus: Compaction behavior, working-set signals, cached compact payloads, and merge/budget logic
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass found that the compaction path should consume WorkingSetTracker rather than transcript heuristics, needs a pre-merge candidate selector before mergeCompactBrief, should strip prior recovered-context markers to avoid feedback loops, and would benefit from a durable latest compact brief alongside the one-shot cache.

focus: Snapshot/export/import, worktree remapping, collision handling, and graph-adjacent contract design
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass identified path-derived graph identity as the main portability blocker, clarified narrow auto-rehome semantics, separated hard structural identity conflicts from soft content dedupe, and established import-finalization as a required stage rather than optional cleanup.

focus: Retention, pinning, blob deduplication, privacy, and preview handling
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass added mechanism detail around lineage-aware leaf-only pruning, hash-after-redaction blob dedupe with backfill, privacy-filtered workspace-bounded previews, and the asymmetry between mature memory archival semantics and still-ephemeral graph/runtime state.

focus: Graph-facing retrieval surfaces, seed resolution, budget shaping, and semantic bridge behavior
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass mapped fine-grained LCM retrieval mechanics onto current graph tools: use seed.query for disambiguation, reduce first-anchor bias in code_graph_context, shift from slice-first to overfetch-then-trim, and add low-signal suppression to the semantic bridge.

focus: Status, health, ensure-ready, stale detection, and self-healing behavior
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass identified inconsistent stale semantics across graph health surfaces, read paths that compute readiness diagnostics and then hide them, parse-health values mislabeled as freshness, and self-healing/debounce behavior that can appear more successful than it is.

focus: OpenCode adapter plugin architecture boundary
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass refined the adapter boundary: the future OpenCode plugin should be a transport facade only, with retrieval policy and diagnostics owned by the runtime and exposed through one adapter-facing composition contract.

focus: Failure modes, regressions, anti-patterns, and hidden costs of over-porting
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass identified concrete over-port risks: competing recovery authorities, duplicate snapshot planes, weaker lexical worktree identity than current path safety, hook-latency regression from inline archive maintenance, and anchor loss from archive-style path redaction.

focus: Dependency-ordered roadmap across adapter and hardening work
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass established a clearer dependency chain: first build a shared payload/provenance composition layer, then the thin OpenCode adapter, then graph/runtime hardening with path/worktree guards before snapshot/doctor and preview/retention later.

focus: Future packet and workstream decomposition
summary: Delegated Copilot GPT-5.4 high pass converged on a 3-packet split: OpenCode transport adapter under 024, public code-graph operations hardening under 024, and memory archive durability outside 024, with doctor preceding path/worktree primitives, then snapshot/rehome, then preview/retention.

focus: Extract live OpenCode plugin loader flow from packed runtime
summary: Packed runtime extraction around src/plugin/index.ts showed the live loader builds a hooks array from internal plus configured plugins, imports configured plugin modules, and invokes each unique module export as a function before pushing the returned hook object.

focus: Map local plugin auto-discovery and file URI conversion
summary: Confirmed the runtime scans {plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js} in config roots, converts discovered files to file:// URIs, and treats local plugin folders as a first-class loading path separate from explicit config.plugin specifiers.

focus: Differentiate explicit plugin specifiers from local file plugins
summary: Confirmed any configured plugin value not starting with file:// is treated as a package specifier and installed through BunProc.install; the bare spec-kit-compact-code-graph entry therefore uses package semantics rather than local-file semantics.

focus: Test exported-module invocation semantics
summary: The live loader does not filter exports by type before invocation and instead calls every unique module export as a function, which makes non-function exports like id unsafe and makes single-function plugin modules the safest compatibility shape.

focus: Compare working plugin patterns from Superset and opencode-lcm
summary: The active Superset plugin and the external opencode-lcm reference both center on a single plugin-function export pattern, reinforcing that mixed metadata-plus-function modules are riskier than the working references suggest.

focus: Trace wrapper/runtime path and active config roots
summary: Confirmed the Superset wrapper sets OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR to ~/.superset/hooks/opencode while launching the Homebrew binary, so live startup behavior reflects wrapper config roots, auto-loaded local plugin folders, and the packed CLI loader together.

focus: Assess version skew between local development packages and live binary
summary: Confirmed version skew between the Homebrew opencode-ai package and the workspace/config-root @opencode-ai/plugin packages, making local type declarations an unreliable proxy for the exact bundled-loader semantics in the live runtime.

focus: Re-evaluate plugin.auth crash path under live loader semantics
summary: The plugin.auth crash best matches an auth-related loop that assumes every hook entry in Plugin.list() is non-null; this keeps the investigation centered on plugin materialization/resolution rather than message-transform payload structure.

focus: Rank likely root causes and invalid assumptions
summary: Most likely remaining causes are mixed export shape, duplicate plugin naming between auto-loaded local file plugins and bare package specifiers, and runtime-version mismatch; the earlier missing-server-export theory alone is no longer sufficient.

focus: Convert loader findings into an implementation-ready fix strategy
summary: Highest-confidence next fix path is to keep a single callable plugin export only, remove non-function metadata exports, and switch away from the bare package-style plugin specifier in favor of local file loading or explicit file:// registration before retesting startup.
